ucs.title: [LLM] TA413 (LuckyCat) FriarFox-style malicious Firefox add-on hijacking Gmail/Tibetan-org sessions
ucs.description: Hunts the TA413 hallmark of dropping a sideloaded Firefox extension (FriarFox / 'Flash update components') that abuses the Firefox WebExtensions APIs to read Gmail messages, cookie jars, and browser credentials of Tibetan diaspora targets. Detects creation/modification of unsigned XPI/extension JSON in Firefox profile paths immediately after delivery from a Tibet-themed lure, paired with anomalous firefox.exe→Google/Gmail egress carrying session cookies.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\extensions\\*", "*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\extension-preferences.json", "*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\addons.json", "*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\extensions.json") (Filesystem.file_name="*.xpi" OR Filesystem.file_name="manifest.json" OR Filesystem.file_name="extensions.json") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","acrord32.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("firefox.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*Scalable Vector Graphics*","*FlashUpdate*","*ff_setup*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.app="firefox.exe" Web.dest IN ("mail.google.com","accounts.google.com","myaccount.google.com") Web.http_user_agent!="*Firefox/*" by Web.src Web.dest Web.url | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | rename src as host] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name file_path file_name dest url
ucs.kql: let LureSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE","AcroRd32.exe") or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("Tibet","CTA","Dalai","TGIE","Free Tibet","Scalable Vector Graphics") | project LureTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine; let ExtDrop = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where FolderPath has @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\" | where FileName endswith ".xpi" or FileName in~ ("manifest.json","addons.json","extensions.json","extension-preferences.json") | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("firefox.exe","updater.exe","maintenanceservice.exe") | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256; let GmailEgress = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "firefox.exe" | where RemoteUrl has_any ("mail.google.com","accounts.google.com","myaccount.google.com","www.googleapis.com/gmail") | summarize GmailHits=count(), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp) by DeviceId, AccountName, RemoteUrl; LureSpawn | join kind=inner ExtDrop on DeviceId | where DropTime between (LureTime .. LureTime + 1h) | join kind=inner GmailEgress on DeviceId | where FirstSeen between (DropTime .. DropTime + 24h) | project LureTime, DropTime, FirstSeen, DeviceId, AccountName, ParentProc, ChildProc, ChildCmd, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, RemoteUrl, GmailHits
ucs.rationale: TA413 is the only documented actor that combined a custom Firefox add-on (FriarFox, observed by Proofpoint Feb-2021) with Tibetan-themed lures to siphon Gmail and Firefox creds. Correlating an Office/Reader/Outlook ancestor → unsigned XPI/manifest.json write under a Firefox profile → firefox.exe egress to Gmail within hours is a behavior chain almost no benign software produces, and it sits square

ucs.title: [LLM] Tropic Trooper / KeyBoy DLL side-loading via signed-binary launcher with MSBuild/Regsvr32 staging
ucs.description: Catches the long-running TA413/KeyBoy/Tropic Trooper pattern of dropping a benign signed executable (e.g. legitimate AV, Office, or system utility) into a writable user path alongside a hijacked DLL (USP10.dll, VERSION.dll, libcurl.dll, log.dll) which loads Sepulcher/USBferry/TClient. Pivots on signed-binary execution from non-standard paths immediately followed by suspicious DLL load and outbound C2 to typical TA413 infrastructure (often abusing Steam/legit web services for T1102.002).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("msbuild.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe") OR (Processes.process_signature_is_valid=true AND Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\Downloads\\*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host, process_id as pid | join type=inner host pid [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("USP10.dll","VERSION.dll","libcurl.dll","log.dll","libcef.dll","vftrace.dll","goopdate.dll","cryptbase.dll") (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host, process_id as pid] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080) All_Traffic.dest IN ("steamcommunity.com","steampowered.com","*.dropbox.com","*.googleusercontent.com","*.amazonaws.com") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as host] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name process_path file_name file_path dest app
ucs.kql: let SideloadDrop = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(30d) | where FileName in~ ("USP10.dll","VERSION.dll","libcurl.dll","log.dll","libcef.dll","vftrace.dll","goopdate.dll","cryptbase.dll","WTSAPI32.dll") | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Users\Public\",@"\Temp\",@"\Downloads\") | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName; let SignedHostExec = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(30d) | where ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Microsoft","Google","Adobe","Kaspersky","Symantec","ESET","VMware","Tencent") | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Users\Public\",@"\Temp\",@"\Downloads\") | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId; let MsbuildOrProxy = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(30d) | where FileName in~ ("msbuild.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","mmc.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Users\Public\",@"\Temp\",".xml",".csproj",".sct") | project ProxyTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ProxyName=FileName, ProxyCmd=ProcessCommandLine; let WebSvcC2 = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(30d) | where RemoteUrl has_any ("steamcommunity.com","steampowered.com","dropbox.com","googleusercontent.com","github.io","pastebin.com") | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Users\Public\",@"\Temp\") or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msbuild.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe") | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath; SideloadDrop | join kind=inner SignedHostExec on DeviceId | where ExecTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 2h) and FolderPath == FolderPath1 | join kind=leftouter MsbuildOrProxy on DeviceId | join kind=inner WebSvcC2 on DeviceId | where NetTime between (ExecTime .. ExecTime + 6h) | project DropTime, ExecTime, NetTime, DeviceId, AccountName, SignedBinary=FileName1, SideloadedDLL=FileName, FolderPath, ProxyName, ProxyCmd, RemoteUrl, SHA256, SHA2561
ucs.rationale: Tropic Trooper/KeyBoy has used DLL search-order hijack against legitimately signed launchers as its primary install primitive for nearly a decade (USBferry, Sepulcher, TClient, FakeNarrator). Pairing this with MSBuild/regsvr32 staging (T1127.001 explicitly listed in their TTPs) and bidirectional C2 over public web services like Steam Community profiles (documented Trend Micro / Talos reporting on
